demographics.living_situation: lives at home with a live-in adult child who provides some care

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I've been home from hospital for two days, I can't even walk to the toilet anymore, and I'm terrified I'm going to end up in a nursing home forever.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The patient and her live-in adult child called because since coming home she has become too frightened and too weak to move around safely, and they want help to keep her safely at home.

symptom_narrative.onset: over the two days since she was discharged from hospital
symptom_narrative.duration: getting worse each day at home
symptom_narrative.character: she feels she has no strength in her legs and is too scared of falling to stand or walk
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: she says she can't get to the bathroom on her own and feels completely stuck
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: her legs feel weak and her knees are painful and stiff
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since discharge, worst when she tries to stand
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: having her adult child right beside her to hold onto
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: trying to move on her own, and thinking about falling
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: overwhelming worry and panic about coping at home; no new chest pain, no fever, no new cough or coloured phlegm beyond her usual
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: she was recently in hospital and says she was much more independent before that admission
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: she is staying in bed almost all the time because she is frightened of falling, and cannot manage getting to the toilet or washing without help

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: severe emphysema / bad lungs (COPD) — was just in hospital for a flare-up
recently discharged from hospital a couple of days ago

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a once-a-day inhaler for her lungs (Tiotropium / Spiriva)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives at home with a live-in adult child who is helping her but is becoming exhausted; the household is worried about her being alone if she can't move safely

communication_style.typical_message_style: frightened, focused on the fear of losing her independence and going into a nursing home

access_barriers.transport: housebound at present — cannot easily attend a clinic because she cannot mobilise safely

objective_data_offered.value: the adult child reports she is staying in bed and will not stand or walk on her own because she is too frightened of falling
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: caregiver observation, not a formal mobility assessment

objective_data_offered.value: on video she looks frail and anxious, sitting up in bed and visibly overwhelmed
the adult child says her breathing looks about the same as her usual and she is speaking in full sentences; no new blue lips
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective caregiver impression, not a measured respiratory assessment

objective_data_offered.value: on the video call the home looks cluttered with items on the floor, and the adult child says there are no rails in the bathroom
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: informal view over video, not a professional home-hazard assessment
